articles.title: THE PROVEN PLAYBOOK FOR SHIFTING TO USAGE BASED PRICING
DEVELOPING A TRANSFORMATIONAL VISION FOR AI AND BEYOND
articles.summary: Companies must envision long-term industry shifts to build a strong AI strategy, aligning with Amara's Law, which highlights our tendency to overestimate short-term impacts but underestimate long-term effects. Businesses can innovate proactively instead of merely following trends by anticipating changes in jobs and customer needs.

articles.title: SCARCITY AND ABUNDANCE IN 2025
articles.summary: Three key themes have emerged in technology's shift from scarcity to abundance: code evolving from capital to labor, AI's relentless Red Queen's Race, and AI agents reshaping economic activity. The greatest impact lies in decentralized, market-driven applications, with onchain agents and stablecoin-based AI transactions leading the way.

articles.title: BEYOND DIRECT SALES
articles.summary: Channel partnerships boost reach and revenue but work best after product-market fit and without sales conflicts. Product managers should choose low-friction partners, streamline enablement, and balance growth with direct sales.

articles.title: IT'S NOT BRAND VS. GROWTH
articles.summary: Brand-market fit is as crucial as product-market fit but often overlooked. Companies like Notion, Figma, and Canva treat their brands as growth engines, driving organic acquisition, engagement, and loyalty from the start.

articles.title: THE ANTI-PATTERN PLAYBOOK
articles.summary: Product teams often ship features aimlessly, driven by noise instead of user needs. To escape this cycle, they must focus on outcomes, run smaller experiments, and strengthen stakeholder relationships.

articles.title: THE 12 TYPES OF PRODUCT MANAGERS: WHAT PRODUCT MANAGER CAN YOU BECOME?
articles.summary: Product managers specialize in areas like Technical, Growth, AI, and Enterprise, each suited to different business needs. Knowing these roles helps PMs align strengths for greater impact and career fulfillment.

articles.title: WHY DON'T OUR LEADERS CARE ABOUT HOW WE WORK?
articles.summary: Most companies undervalue intentional operating system design, defaulting instead to ad hoc processes shaped by urgency, habit, and limited awareness. Without leadership buy-in or systems-thinking skills, change efforts often stall amid skepticism, inertia, and misaligned incentives.

articles.title: CREATIVITY AS YOUR LEADERSHIP ADVANTAGE IN GENAI LEADERSHIP
articles.summary: GenAI accelerates innovation, making creativity the key competitive edge as execution speeds up. With software development no longer a differentiator, leaders must cultivate creativity to captivate customers and stay ahead.

articles.title: WHY ARE GOOGLE'S AI PRODUCTS SO FRUSTRATING TO USE?
articles.summary: Google's AI products suffer from confusing UX, fragmentation, and hidden features.

articles.title: USE "BUT" STRATEGICALLY
articles.summary: The "Inverted But Technique" improves communication by flipping negative and positive statements, making feedback more encouraging.
